title: Theol Drost
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Theol_Drost2.jpg]] |name=Theol Drost |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[The Last Command 3|''The Last Command'' 3]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]''[[The Last Command]]'' |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Blue]] |skin=Light |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]{{WEGCite|story=Picutorion Viewed From the Top|book=Imperial Sourcebook|reprint=1}} **[[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] **[[Thrawn's confederation]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Drost is a good enough man in his way. His chief weakness is a tendency to become complacent. For the immediate future, at least, he should be cured of that.|Grand Admiral Thrawn, to Captain Gilad Pellaeon|}} '''Theol Drost''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] serving the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. As a [[High Colonel/Legends|high colonel]], Drost commanded the Army detachment committed to the [[Raid on Picutorion|assault]] on [[Picutorion/Legends|Picutorion]], an engagement that was part of the [[Kwymar Suppressions/Legends|Kwymar Suppressions]], alongside his [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] counterpart, [[Commodore/Legends|Commodore]] [[Bevven]]. During the battle, Drost deferred to the judgment of [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]] [[Officer/Legends|officer]] [[Mar Barezz]] in allowing two of his [[Soldier/Legends|men]], [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Ganig]] and [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Stecker]], to be [[Death/Legends|killed]] in action to prevent the possibility of two decorated heroes of the Empire defecting to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. Drost spent the next [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] commanding various [[Space station/Legends|space installations]] until receiving a promotion to [[General/Legends|general]] and command of the security of the [[Bilbringi Shipyards]]. In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], Bilbringi fell under the aegis of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] [[Thrawn's confederation|forces]] and was responsible for building new [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]]. A [[Raid on Bilbringi|raid]] on the facility conducted by the [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] [[Mazzic]] and [[Ellor]] resulted in the destruction of a nearly-completed Star Destroyer and revealed flaws in General Drost's security setup. Rather than punish the general, Grand [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Thrawn gave Drost thirty [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] to revise Bilbringi's security system. Drost's revisions did not, however, stand up to an [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|assault]] on the [[Bilbringi system/Legends|Bilbringi system]] by a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], and he was removed from command shortly after. ==Biography== ===Picutorion=== {{Quote|How long could your men hold out in their present position, colonel?''"
"''Normally I would say less than three minutes. They are good. I would guess seven.|Commodore Bevven and High Colonel Theol Drost|}} Theol Drost was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]]''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]'' who served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He commanded the Imperial Army element committed to the [[Raid on Picutorion|assault]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Picutorion/Legends|Picutorion]], which was part of the [[Kwymar Suppressions/Legends|Kwymar Suppressions]],{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/huntwithin1|text=The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale: Part 1|int=The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale: Part 1}} in [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]].{{SW|url=news/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-11-serving-the-empire|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 11: Serving the Empire}} establishes that the [[Kwymar Suppressions/Legends|Kwymar Suppressions]] take place in [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]]. Drost held the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[High Colonel/Legends|high colonel]] during the engagement, alongside [[Commodore/Legends|Commodore]] [[Bevven]], who headed the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] contingent. Both commanded the battle from the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] of Bevven's [[Starship/Legends|starship]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of Picutorion. During the battle, elements of the [[armor battalion]] assigned to [[Tensiger]]'s [[6th regiment]] penetrated the defenses of an [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]–held [[tracking station]], but Alliance resistance was fierce with [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighters]] flying in support of the defensive action. Drost and Bevven discussed the possibility of air support for the ground assault; Drost knew the attack would falter without Imperial air cover, but Bevven was afraid that the Y-wings would choose to attack the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] and wanted to send [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] in a wave attack while keeping the bulk of the [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] in reserve. Drost believed that a piecemeal effort, one that only eliminated a handful of fighters with every pass, would effectively doom the [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] on the ground. The TIEs, however, were solely under Bevven's command. As Bevven began to order the TIEs to launch on attack runs, he and Drost were interrupted by [[Mar Barezz]], an [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] of the [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]]. Barezz relayed information to the pair that two of the soldiers attacking the tracking station—[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Ganig]] and [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Stecker]]—had been tagged as "sensitive" by the Bureau. Both had been decorated by the [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|Commission for the Preservation of the New Order]] for their actions at the [[Battle of Sagma]], but their lax attitude toward Imperial doctrine had been highlighted. In addition, they had been in contact with another soldier named [[Linds]], whom the Bureau was confident was an Alliance sympathizer. Barezz suggested that any air support for the soldiers be delayed until both Ganig and Stecker were [[Death/Legends|dead]]; although there was no proof that either man planned to defect to the Rebellion, Barezz felt the risk was too high. Both would be decorated by COMPNOR again if they captured the tracking station, and Barezz believed that dead heroes were just as good as live ones for [[Propaganda/Legends|propaganda]] purposes. Bevven looked to Drost for assent; while Bevven commanded the fighters, the soldiers on the ground were Drost's. The high colonel agreed with the plan, and the starfighter assault was delayed by ten [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]]. ===Later career=== [[File:TheolDrost-EGPM.png|thumb|right|180px|General Theol Drost]] {{Quote|Have you anything to add to this report, General Drost?''"
"''No, sir.''"
"''Can you offer me any reason why you should not be relieved of command?''"
"''No, sir.''"
"''Return to your headquarters, General. The ''Chimaera'' will be leaving here in approximately thirty hours. You have until then to design and implement a new security system for the shipyards. At that point I'll make my decision about your future.''"
"''Understood, sir. I won't fail you again, Admiral.|Grand Admiral Thrawn and General Theol Drost|}} Drost proved to be an adept tactician in matters surrounding [[Realspace/Legends|space]]-based Imperial installations and served as commander of various outposts, including fueling centers, ammo dumps, and [[Space station/Legends|space station]]–based [[Garrison/Legends|garrisons]]. Eventually, Drost received a promotion to [[General/Legends|general]] and was assigned as [[Chief of Security/Legends|security chief]] of the [[Bilbringi Shipyards]]. Drost's headquarters were located on [[Bilbringi VII]], a [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]] with no [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] that was covered in signal relays and observation towers. Drost knew that his assignment was important and defended the [[Bilbringi system/Legends|Bilbringi system]] with four [[Golan II Space Defense SpaceGun]]s and a network of [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield barriers]] that prevented unauthorized travel into restricted areas. All incoming [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]] were thoroughly checked before being allowed to proceed.''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' the Bilbringi [[Shipyard/Legends|Shipyards]] were part of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] [[Thrawn's confederation|confederation]] of Imperial forces, which included the Empire proper, the [[Pentastar Alignment]], and the [[Ciutric Hegemony]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' During [[Thrawn campaign|Thrawn's campaign]] against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]—successor state to the Alliance to Restore the Republic and the new dominant [[Politics/Legends|political]] government in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] following the fragmentation of the Empire in the wake of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]—the Bilbringi Shipyards were [[Raid on Bilbringi|raided]] by the [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] [[Mazzic]] and [[Ellor]] in retribution for an Imperial [[Ambush on Trogan|assault]] on a smuggler meeting held at the [[Whistler's Whirlpool Tapcafe]] on the planet [[Trogan/Legends|Trogan]]. The raid resulted in the destruction of an almost complete [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]]. Thrawn arrived at Bilbringi and questioned Drost over the incident aboard his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']], but the general was unable to provide any reasons as to why his failure to ensure the shipyards' security should not result in his removal from command. Thrawn, however, did not immediately punish Drost, giving him thirty [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] to draft and implement a new security system for the installation before deciding his fate. Drost's efforts satisfied Thrawn, but a later [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|assault]] by the New Republic in an effort to capture a [[crystal gravfield trap]] array showed that the general's revisions for the installation's security had been inadequate. The New Republic won the battle after Thrawn was [[Assassin/Legends|assassinated]] by his own [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]], [[Rukh/Legends|Rukh]], and the withdrawal of his fleet, now under the command of Captain [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]]. Drost was removed from command, and the shipyards were effectively abandoned, falling into the hands of the New Republic shortly after.''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]'' ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|What is your point, Barezz?''"
"''My point, colonel, is that for propaganda purposes, dead heroes are nearly as good ''[sic]'' live ones. And dead heroes cannot defect to the Rebellion.''"
"''Do your sources indicate that they are thinking of defecting?''"
"''No, but we are certain that a trooper named Linds is a Rebel sympathizer. He has been in contact with each man.|High Colonel Drost and Mar Barezz|}} Theol Drost was a light-skinned, [[Color/Legends|blue]]-[[Eye/Legends|eyed]] man with a barrel chest and a short, stocky build. Physically fit and capable, Drost was a formidable-looking man who reminded his fellows of a [[Commando/Legends|commando]]. An able commander, Drost respected the abilities of the soldiers under his command. In dealings with the Imperial Security Bureau officer Mar Barezz, he was cautious around the man and held the officer's gaze but did not go against his suggestion to delay starfighter support to allow possible defectors to perish in battle. Drost was not one for excuses; when faced by Grand Admiral Thrawn over his failure to secure the Bilbringi Shipyards, Drost did not attempt to excuse his actions and was prepared to face any fate the Grand Admiral deemed appropriate. ==Skills and abilities== Drost was a capable Army commander, knowledgeable in [[Ground warfare/Legends|ground assault]] tactics, space installation security procedures, and perimeter defense. During the Battle of Picutorion, he held the rank of high colonel, which was commonly associated with command of an Army [[Battle group/Legends|battle group]].''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'' Drost was trained in the use of a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and [[Unarmed combat/Legends|unarmed combat]] as well as first aid. He was also skilled in the operation of [[Walker/Legends|walkers]] and beast riding. Drost's [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of working as an installation commander gave him knowledge about the internal workings and procedures of a [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucracy]]. ==Equipment== Drost wore a standard Imperial Army [[Imperial officer's uniform/Legends|uniform]] and carried a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]] and [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]]. ==Behind the scenes== Theol Drost first appeared in "[[Picutorion Viewed From the Top]]," a short story first published in [[West End Games]]' [[1989]] first edition of the ''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'' reference title, where he was simply identified as Drost. The character's first name, Theol, was revealed in West End Games' [[1994]] ''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]'', and he was given his first visual depiction in the [[The Last Command 3|third issue]] of the [[Star Wars: The Last Command (comic series)|''The Last Command'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] adaptation of the [[The Last Command|eponymous novel]]. With illustrations by [[Edvin Biuković|Edvin Biukovic]], the comic was released by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[February 4]], [[1998]].{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54613/star_wars_the_last_command_1997_3|text=Star Wars: The Last Command (1997) #3}} ==Appearances== *{{WEGCite|story=Picutorion Viewed From the Top|book=Imperial Sourcebook|reprint=1}} {{1st}} *''[[The Last Command]]'' {{Ab|The Last Command (unabridged audiobook)}} *[[The Last Command 3|''The Last Command'' 3]] ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Theol Drost}} *''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]'' {{1stID|Theol}} *''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Theol Drost }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Drost, Theol}} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Army generals of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Males]]
